summary.intent: Make the auto-continue minimum tool-call threshold configurable instead of hardcoded to 3, following a PR review suggestion.
summary.outcome: Added `auto_continue_min_tool_calls` as a configurable field (default 3) in schema, template, and validate; replaced the hardcoded `3` in the agent runner with the config value; committed and pushed to the PR.

repo: Auto-continue behavior lives in crates/agents/src/runner.rs and is gated on total_tool_calls >= N and a max_auto_continues check.
Config changes require touching four files: schema.rs (field definition + doc-comment), template.rs (default/template output), validate.rs (validation logic), and the consuming crate (runner.rs).
The project uses i18n via a t() helper — user-visible strings should use t('namespace:key', 'fallback') rather than bare string literals.

code.finding: New field auto_continue_min_tool_calls added here with doc-comment explaining the threshold semantics.
Template updated to emit the new field with its default value of 3.
Validation logic extended for the new field.
Both the main loop and streaming loop replaced hardcoded `3` with config-sourced auto_continue_min_tool_calls; test comment updated accordingly.

workflow: PR review comments were addressed incrementally across multiple commits rather than squashed, keeping the review history readable.
Background bash tasks (commit + push) were used to avoid blocking the main conversation thread.

open_items: Consider whether auto_continue_min_tool_calls needs a documented recommended range or upper-bound validation to prevent nonsensical values.
